pattern: Check each column independently: if any adjacent row pair is out of order in that column, the column must be deleted.
hints: Think of the strings as rows of a character grid. For each column index, examine every pair of vertically adjacent characters.
A column is unsorted if strs[i][col] > strs[i+1][col] for any row i. Count such columns.
Iterate over columns in the outer loop and rows in the inner loop; bail early per column as soon as you find a violation.

edge_inputs.description: single string, no adjacent pair, zero deletions
equal characters in a column are allowed (not strictly decreasing)
two strings, second column violated only

stress.description: 100 strings each of length 1000 with random lowercase letters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, size)
    w = 1000
    strs = [''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(w)) for _ in range(n)]
    return [strs]
